data.name: Prathamesh Palatshaha
data.summary: Analytical background with hands-on project work in financial analysis, reporting, and data visualization using Python, SQL, and Power BI.

experience.description: Data Analysis: Analyzed large operational datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and performance drivers. Automation: Built Python pipelines for preprocessing, validation, and analysis, reducing manual effort. Model Evaluation: Applied ensemble techniques achieving 99.85% accuracy using precision, recall, ROC-AUC. Stakeholder Communication: Presented insights to cross-functional teams to support operational decisions.

projects.name: Automated Financial Analysis & Valuation Modeling
projects.description: Built an automated financial analysis and valuation framework for HSBC Holdings using Python. Analyzed historical financial performance of HSBC Holdings over a 19-year period (2005–2024). Identified operational efficiency improvement as cost-to-income ratio declined from 73.6% (2016) to approximately 50.3% (2024). Evaluated profitability recovery through ROE analysis, increasing from approximately 1.4% (2016) to 12.96% (2024). Performed CAGR analysis showing divergence between profitability and revenue: Net Income CAGR +27.89% versus Revenue CAGR 0.96%. Applied relative valuation using P/E multiples under scenario-based assumptions: 7× (Bear), 8× (Base), and 9× (Bull). Estimated implied equity valuation range of approximately $167B–$215B, with base-case valuation around $191.8B.

projects.name: Customer Shopping Behavior Analysis
projects.description: Analyzed customer-level data for 3,900 individuals, maintaining a $59.76 average purchase value and 3.75/5 rating. Performed data processing across 4 product categories, identifying Clothing as the market leader with over $100K in revenue. Engineered features to track demographics, finding Young Adults generated the highest revenue at $62,143. Used SQL queries to uncover a revenue gap where Male customers ($157K) doubled the contribution of Females ($75K). Built a Power BI dashboard visualizing that 80% (3,116) of customers are in the Loyal segment. Developed an analytical report identifying Jeans as the underperforming product category, with a low average customer rating of 3.65 and a high discount rate of 31%.

projects.name: Banking Data Analysis & Dashboard
projects.description: Analyzed banking datasets to study customer behavior and transaction trends. Cleaned and structured raw CSV and Excel data for database ingestion. Imported data into MySQL and queried it using Python for analysis. Performed exploratory data analysis using pandas, matplotlib, and seaborn. Identified key metrics and dimensions for reporting and dashboard design. Built an interactive Power BI dashboard with filters, KPIs, and visual charts.

education.institution: Dr B R Ambedkar National Institute of Technology

education.degree: B.E. Computer Engineering (Honours in Data Science)

data.domain: Data Science, Machine Learning, Financial Analysis
